{{Quote|Full forward. Flatten those soldiers.|Lieutenant Nyrox|<ref name="GG3, First Edition" />}}
'''Nyrox''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A product of the elite training program at [[Raithal Academy/Legends|Raithal Academy]], Nyrox demonstrated his skill and ingenuity in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of the giant [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]]. Having caught the attention of the [[Walker/Legends|walker]] line's main proponent, [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], Nyrox graduated from the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] and was promptly promoted to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]. Veers selected Nyrox to [[Vehicle Command Crewer|pilot]] the AT-AT walker [[Thunderer One]], the lead walker of [[Thundering Herd/Legends|Thundering Herd]], his personal AT-AT unit. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Nyrox served under Veers at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], leading the invading walker [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] to victory over the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] defenders of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]]. He earned the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] for his actions.

==Biography==
===Imperial academy and training===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="GG3, First Edition" /> Nyrox was a graduate of the prestigious [[Raithal Academy/Legends|Raithal Academy]], a training branch of the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], where he trained in the [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground warfare]] tactics of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]. Nyrox was instructed in all types of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Army/Legends|Army]] hardware, ranging from ground-based [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] to complex vehicles like the [[HAVw A5 Juggernaut]], the [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|HAVr A9 Floating Fortress]], and [[PX-4 Mobile Command Base]]s. His weapon of choice, however, was the mammoth [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]]. Nyrox relished the sense of power the [[Walker/Legends|walker]] afforded him.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

[[File:Maximilian_Veers.jpg|thumb|Maximilian Veers, who witnessed Nyrox's skills]]
He demonstrated his ability in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of the AT-AT with such flair that it caught the attention of [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], the premiere supporter of walker technology. During an inspection tour of the Raithal Academy sometime during the formation of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], the Imperial [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], Veers witnessed Nyrox's skill first-hand. Nyrox was running a full-immersion [[Flight simulator/Legends|holo-simulation]] against a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] supported by [[Ultra-Light Assault Vehicle/Legends|Ultra-Light Assault Vehicles]] and [[Freerunner/Legends|Freerunners]]. When a lucky hit crippled one of his AT-AT's legs, Nyrox resourcefully decided to take up a defensive crouching posture. Although the maneuver ground the walker's bearings to the very hub, he was able to destroy each Rebel craft by making them come to him.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

===Thunderer One and Battle of Hoth===
[[File:Crossfire_EaW.png|thumb|left|250px|The Battle of Hoth, where Nyrox's actions led to his promotion]]
Most impressed with the trainee's simulation, Veers appointed Nyrox as [[Vehicle Command Crewer|pilot]] for his lead walker, [[Thunderer One]], the point walker of [[Thundering Herd/Legends|Thundering Herd]], an elite AT-AT force under his command. Nyrox's abilities earned him the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] straight out of the Academy.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> During his time at Raithal Academy, he filed a report at the Raithal Academy on understanding and preparing for enemy exploitation of the AT-AT's vulnerabilities, and it was later included in the [[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide|''Imperial Handbook'']] guide for Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commanders]].<ref name="Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

Nyrox served with Veers through action at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the Imperial campaign to crush the Rebel Alliance [[Alliance High Command/Legends|headquarters]] of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] in<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> during which he [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the invasion's lead walker. For his actions during the Rebel defeat at [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], Nyrox was promoted to [[Captain/Legends|captain]].<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

==Personality and traits==
Like many Imperial personnel, Nyrox enjoyed a sense of power and control over his opposition, which is what influenced his taste for the might of the AT-AT. He possessed a certain level of military tactical prowess and flair, opting to assume a defensive position with his AT-AT during a training exercise after the walker suffered damage to one of its legs, and allowing the enemy craft of his groundbreaking simulation to come to him rather than to seek them out. His skill was well employed during the Battle of Hoth, during which he led the invasion's lead walker in routing the Rebel defenders of Echo Base. Nyrox stood 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

==Skills and abilities==
As an Imperial Army pilot, Nyrox possessed [[Technician/Legends|mechanical ability]] in the operation of ground vehicles, [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] craft, walkers,<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> and AT-AT [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]].<ref name="GG3, First Edition" /> He also had specific technical knowledge in AT-AT walker repair.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

==Equipment==
Nyrox's equipment included a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and battle [[Armor/Legends|armor]], with an internal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], that covered his head and chest.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Nyrox was created in the [[1989]] first edition of the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''.<ref name="GG3, First Edition" /> The same image used for Nyrox was later reused to represent an Imperial [[land assault trooper]] in [[1991]]'s ''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'', another West End Games sourcebook.<ref name="TC">''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]''</ref> The [[July]] [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' included a detailed biographical profile of Nyrox as part of a major expansion on the text by [[Pablo Hidalgo]].<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" />

===Inconsistencies===
[[File:Blizzard_1.png|thumb|right|200px|Blizzard 1 (pictured), which this article treats as separate from Nyrox's Thunderer One]]
The second edition of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' explains that Nyrox piloted Thunderer One, the lead walker of Veers' elite Thundering Herd AT-AT squadron, the walker squadron assigned to the Battle of Hoth. This implies that Thundering Herd is the AT-AT unit seen in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and that Thunderer One is the AT-AT seen commanded by Veers himself, which would also seem to imply that Nyrox<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> is the pilot seen alongside Veers in the AT-AT cockpit in the film.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> However, the subsequent [[November]] 1996 ''[[Hoth Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] identified Veers' lead walker as "[[Blizzard 1/Legends|Blizzard 1]]," a part of [[Blizzard Force/Legends|Blizzard Force]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's elite assault unit,<ref name="Hoth Limited">{{CCG|set=Hoth Limited|cardname=Blizzard 1}}</ref> whereas ''Galaxy Guide 3'' did not necessarily place the AT-ATs of Thundering Herd as part of Blizzard Force.<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> With no further [[in-universe]] connection made, this article assumes that the Thunderer One and Blizzard 1 walkers are separate entities.

Nyrox's tactic of assuming a defensive, kneeling posture with his AT-AT is strikingly similar<ref name="GG3, Second Edition" /> to the tactic employed by a promising Imperial [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] named [[Davin Felth/Legends|Davin Felth]] in the short story "[[When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale]]," as part of the [[1995]] anthology book ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''. However, in the story, rather than rewarding Felth as he does Nyrox, Veers reassigns Felth to the Imperial [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] to mask an exposed weakness pointed out by Felth regarding the AT-AT design.<ref name="Stormtrooper's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> The ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', released in [[2014]], includes a report filed by "Captain" Nyrox, but the book is said to have been published in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Handbook" /> several years before Nyrox became a captain.<ref name="NEC" />
